basic_details.job_title: SBI Law Officer Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 49 Assistant Manager, Deputy Manager Posts
basic_details.organization_name: State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai
basic_details.post_name: Assistant Manager (Law) – JMGS-I, Deputy Manager (Law) – MMGS-II
basic_details.total_vacancies: 49* (20 – Assistant Manager + 29 – Deputy Manager incl. 2 Backlog) (*subject to change)
basic_details.short_information: State Bank of India (SBI), Central Recruitment & Promotion Department, Corporate Centre, Mumbai has published the Specialist Cadre Officer (SCO) Recruitment Notification 2026-27 vide Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2026-27/02 dated 24.06.2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 49 vacancies (including backlog) for Assistant Manager (Law) – JMGS-I and Deputy Manager (Law) – MMGS-II posts on a Regular Basis. Candidates holding a Law Degree (LLB/5-year integrated) from a recognized university with 02 years (Assistant Manager) or 04 years (Deputy Manager) of mandatory experience as a practicing advocat

application_fee.details: The application fee and intimation charges are non-refundable. The fee must be paid online through the payment gateway using Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking. Application fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be adjusted for any other examination or selection.

age_limit.details: The crucial date for age calculation is 31.05.2026. Age relaxation is admissible to SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates as per Government of India guidelines. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. No change in category is permitted after registration of online application.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Graduate with a degree in Law or a Law Graduate from a recognized university in India.
Graduate with a degree in Law or a Law Graduate from a recognized university in India.

qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 31.05.2026. Educational qualification prescribed is the minimum requirement. Candidates must possess the post-basic qualification and relevant full-time experience as on the specified date. Experience gained during internship and teaching/training experience will NOT be counted for eligibility. The relevant experience certificate from the employer must specifically state that the candidate had experience in the related field as required. | Educational Qualification | Work Experience (Mandatory as on 31.05.2026)

selection_process: Type of Test Maximum Marks
Objective Test 50
Descriptive Test 50
Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time Nature
Test of Reasoning 25 25* 45 Minutes Qualifying only* (marks not counted for merit)
English Language 35 35*
Professional Knowledge 50 50 45 Minutes Counted for Merit
Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Time
Descriptive Test Answer any 05 out of 08 questions 50 (10 x 5) 45 Minutes
Stage Marks Weightage in Final Merit
Online Written Test 100 (Objective 50 + Descriptive 50) 70%
Interview 100 30%
Subject Area
Constitutional Law – Fundamental concepts, issues and current developments
Administrative Law – Fundamental concepts, issues and current developments
Financial Sector Laws – Fundamental concepts, issues and current developments
Procedural Laws – Fundamental concepts, issues and current developments
New Labour Codes – Fundamental concepts, issues and current developments
Principles of Statutory Interpretation and Legal Drafting
Tie-Break In case more than one candidate scores the same cut-off marks in the final merit list, such candidates will be ranked according to their age in descending order.

The selection will be based on Online Written Test followed by Interview. The Online Written Test will be conducted tentatively in August 2026. Call letters will be uploaded on the Bank's website and intimated via SMS/email. Final Merit list will be prepared based on normalization of marks scored in Online Written Test and Interview with 70:30 weightage respectively.
Online Written Test Pattern
Objective Test Pattern

*General Aptitude (Reasoning + English) is qualifying in nature only. Marks will not be counted for merit. Only Professional Knowledge marks will be reckoned for arriving at merit. There will be NO negative marks for wrong answers in the Online Written Examination.
Descriptive Test Pattern

Candidates need to qualify in both Objective Test and Descriptive Test individually. Merit list will be prepared based on total score in Objective Test (Professional Knowledge) and Descriptive Test combined.
Interview
Category-wise adequate number of candidates will be called for Interview based on performance in Online Written Test. Qualifying marks in Interview will be as decided by the Bank.
Indicative Syllabus for Professional Knowledge (Law)
Note: Syllabus mentioned is only indicative and not exhaustive. Online Examination may contain questions beyond the purview of the syllabus as deemed fit by the Bank.
Tie-Breaking Rule
how_to_apply: Candidates must apply online through the Bank's official website at https://sbi.bank.in/web/careers/current-openings. No other mode of application will be entertained. Hard copy of application and other documents need not be sent to the office. The process of registration is completed only when the fee is deposited with the Bank through online mode on or before the last date for fee payment.

Candidates are advised to apply online well before the closing date and not to wait till the last date to avoid disconnection/inability/failure to log on to the website due to heavy load. After final submission, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances.
Documents to be Uploaded at the Time of Online Application:
Recent passport size colour photograph (JPG/JPEG, 20–50 kb, 200x230 pixels preferred)
Live photograph (via webcam or mobile scan)
Signature (on white paper with Black ink pen; JPG/JPEG, 10–20 kb, 140x60 pixels; NOT in capital letters)
Brief Resume (PDF)
ID Proof (PDF)
Proof of Date of Birth (PDF)
Educational Certificates: Relevant Marksheets / Degree Certificate (PDF)
Proof of enrolment with Bar Council (PDF)
Experience Certificates (PDF)
Caste Certificate / EWS Certificate (if applicable) (PDF)
PwBD Certificate (if applicable) (PDF)
Preferred Qualification / Certification (if any) (PDF)
Form-16 / Offer Letter / Latest Salary Slip from current employer (PDF)
Documents to be Produced at the Time of Interview (if called):
All original documents uploaded during online application
Valid caste certificate in prescribed Government of India format (for reserved category candidates)
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) Certificate issued between 01.04.2026 and the date of interview
Income & Asset Certificate (EWS) for FY 2025-26 in prescribed format from Competent Authority
PwBD Disability Certificate dated on or before the last date of application registration
No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Govt./Quasi Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks)
Discharge Certificate/Relieving Certificate from current employer (at the time of joining, if selected)
Photo identity proof in original (Passport/Aadhar/PAN Card/Driving License/Voter's Card/Bank Passbook with attested photograph)
SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27: Apply Here
important_instructions: Online applications only – no other mode of application will be accepted; no hard copy to be sent to the Bank. Candidates are NOT allowed to apply for more than one post. In case of multiple applications for the same post, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited. Candidates must have a valid Email ID and Mobile Number active till the declaration of result and final selection. After expiry of the online application window, no change/correction/modification will be allowed under any circumstances. Admission to all stages (Online Test, Shortlisting, Interview) will be purely provisional subject to satisfying the prescribed eligibility conditions – qualifying in Online Test/Shortlisting is provisional and candidature is subject to document verification at interview. Internship experience and teaching/training experience will NOT be counted for eligibility. CIBIL: Candidates who have defaulted in repayment of loans/credit card dues with Banks/NBFCs/Financial Institutions and have not regularized/repaid the outstanding by the date of issuance of offer of recruitment shall NOT be eligible for appointment. Candidates serving in Govt./Quasi Govt./PSU/Nationalized Banks must submit NOC from employer at the time of interview; failing which candidature will not be considered. OBC-NCL candidates must submit certificate issued between 01.04.2026 and date of interview; EWS candidates must have Income & Asset Certificate for FY 2025-26 on or before last date of online application. Outstation candidates called for interview will be reimbursed economy class airfare (shortest route) up to a maximum of Rs. 10,000 (to-and-fro) or actual travel cost (whichever is lower) on submission of original flight tickets/invoice and boarding pass. Local transport will not be reimbursed. All Changes/Updates/Corrigendum/Results/Schedules/List of shortlisted/selected candidates will be hosted on the Bank's website only. No hard copy of call letter/advice will be sent by post. Candidates furnishing false information/suppressing facts will be disqualified and shall be liable for debarment and legal/criminal action. SBI does not endorse, authorize, or associate with any external coaching platform, consultancy, individual, or digital channel claiming to provide guaranteed selection, influence in recruitment, or insider guidance. Candidates must rely solely on information available on SBI's official career portal. All legal proceedings shall be subject to jurisdiction of Courts/Tribunals/Forums at Mumbai only. For any query, write through the "Contact Us" link at https://sbi.bank.in/web/careers/post-your-query

content_sections.heading: SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27 Important Dates

Last Date for Online Application & Fee Payment

content_sections.heading: SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27 - Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 49 vacancies (including 2 backlog vacancies for Deputy Manager) have been announced for Assistant Manager (Law) and Deputy Manager (Law) posts. The vacancies are distributed across SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and UR categories. PwBD reservation is horizontal and is included within the overall vacancy of the respective parent category. The number of vacancies including reserved vacancies is provisional and may vary as per actual requirements of the Bank. Abbreviations: JMGS – Junior Management Grade Scale; MMGS – Middle Management Grade Scale; SC – Scheduled Caste; ST – Scheduled Tribe; OBC – Other Backward Classes (Non-Creamy Layer); EWS – Economically Weaker Sections; UR – Unreserved; PwBD – Persons with Benchmark Disabilities; VI – Visually Impaired; HI – Hearing Impaired; LD – Locomotor Disability.

content_sections.heading: SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions as on the cut-off date 31.05.2026. Educational qualification prescribed is the minimum requirement. Candidates must possess the post-basic qualification and relevant full-time experience as on the specified date. Experience gained during internship and teaching/training experience will NOT be counted for eligibility. The relevant experience certificate from the employer must specifically state that the candidate had experience in the related field as required. Educational Qualification Work Experience (Mandatory as on 31.05.2026)

rows.mandatory_qualification: Graduate with a degree in Law or a Law Graduate from a recognized university in India.
rows.preferred_qualification: 5-year integrated law course from a recognized university in India may be given preference.

rows.mandatory_qualification: Graduate with a degree in Law or a Law Graduate from a recognized university in India.
rows.preferred_qualification: 5-year integrated law course from a recognized university in India may be given preference.

rows.mandatory_experience: Law graduates who have enrolled as Advocates with Bar Council and have 02 years of experience as practicing advocate. OR 02 years of experience as Law Officer in the Legal Department of Scheduled Commercial Banks. OR 02 years of combined experience as practicing advocate and Law Officer in the Legal Department of Scheduled Commercial Banks. (Experience should be after enrolment as advocate with Bar Council.)

Law graduates who have enrolled as Advocates with Bar Council and have 04 years of experience as practicing advocate. OR 04 years of experience as Law Officer in the Legal Department of Scheduled Commercial Banks. OR 04 years of combined experience as practicing advocate and Law Officer in the Legal Department of Scheduled Commercial Banks. (Experience should be after enrolment as advocate with Bar Council.)

content_sections.heading: SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: The crucial date for age calculation is 31.05.2026. Age relaxation is admissible to SC/ST, OBC, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen candidates as per Government of India guidelines. Cumulative age relaxation will not be available. No change in category is permitted after registration of online application.

rows.category: Ex-Servicemen / ECOs / SSCOs (with 5 years military service, released on completion of assignment)

Application fee once paid will NOT be refunded on any account nor can it be adjusted for any other examination or selection.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: A total of 49 vacancies have been announced – 20 for Assistant Manager (Law) on JMGS-I grade and 29 for Deputy Manager (Law) on MMGS-II grade (including 2 ST backlog vacancies). The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary as per actual requirements of the Bank.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application and pay the fee for SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27 is 14 July 2026. The application window opened on 24 June 2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date to avoid last-minute technical issues.

faq.question: Q3. What is the eligibility/experience required for SBI Assistant Manager (Law) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Law degree (LLB) or be a Law Graduate from a recognized university and must have 02 years of mandatory experience – either as a practicing advocate (enrolled with Bar Council), or as a Law Officer in the Legal Department of a Scheduled Commercial Bank, or a combination of both. Internship and teaching experience will NOT be counted. Age: 25–32 years as on 31.05.2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility/experience required for SBI Deputy Manager (Law) post?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Law degree (LLB) from a recognized university and must have 04 years of mandatory experience – either as a practicing advocate (enrolled with Bar Council), or as a Law Officer in the Legal Department of a Scheduled Commercial Bank, or a combination of both. Internship and teaching experience will NOT be counted. Age: 25–35 years as on 31.05.2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: General, EWS, and OBC candidates must pay Rs. 750/- (non-refundable). SC, ST, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any application fee or intimation charges. Payment must be made online through Debit Card/Credit Card/Internet Banking.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: The selection consists of an Online Written Test (Objective Test of 50 marks + Descriptive Test of 50 marks) followed by an Interview of 100 marks. The final merit list is prepared with 70% weightage to Online Written Test and 30% weightage to Interview scores. General Aptitude (Reasoning + English) in the Objective Test is qualifying in nature only.

faq.question: Q7. Is there negative marking in the SBI SCO Law Officer Written Exam 2026?
faq.answer: No. There will be NO negative marking for wrong answers in the Online Written Examination for SBI SCO Law Officer Recruitment 2026-27.

faq.question: Q8. What is the syllabus for the SBI SCO Law Officer Professional Knowledge paper?
faq.answer: The indicative syllabus for Professional Knowledge covers fundamental concepts, issues, and current developments in Constitutional Law, Administrative Law, Financial Sector Laws, Procedural Laws, New Labour Codes, and Principles of Statutory Interpretation and Legal Drafting. The syllabus is indicative only and the exam may contain questions beyond the listed topics.

faq.question: Q9. What is the pay scale offered for SBI SCO Law Officer 2026-27?
faq.answer: Assistant Manager (Law) – JMGS-I: Basic Pay 48480-2000/7-62480-2340/2-67160-2680/7-85920. Deputy Manager (Law) – MMGS-II: Basic Pay 64820-2340/1-67160-2680/10-93960. Officers are also eligible for DA, HRA, CCA, Provident Fund, NPS, Leave Fare Concession, Medical Facility, and other perquisites as per rules.

faq.question: Q10. Can a candidate apply for both Assistant Manager (Law) and Deputy Manager (Law) posts in SBI SCO 2026-27?
faq.answer: No. Candidates are NOT allowed to apply for more than one post. If multiple applications are submitted, only the last valid (completed) application will be retained and the fee paid for other registrations will stand forfeited. Multiple attendance at the interview will result in rejection of candidature.
